lines.english: The True Guru, the Giver, grants liberation;

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭਿ ਰੋਗ ਗਵਾਏ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਰਸੁ ਪਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: sabh rog gavaae amrit ras paae |
lines.english: all diseases are eradicated, and one is blessed with the Ambrosial Nectar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਮੁ ਜਾਗਾਤਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਕਰੁ ਲਾਗੈ ਜਿਸੁ ਅਗਨਿ ਬੁਝੀ ਠਰੁ ਸੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: jam jaagaat naahee kar laagai jis agan bujhee tthar seenaa he |5|
lines.english: Death, the tax collector, does not impose any tax on one whose inner fire has been put out, whose heart is cool and tranquil. ||5||
The body has developed a great love for the soul-swan.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਹੁ ਜੋਗੀ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਓਹ ਸੁੰਦਰਿ ਨਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: ohu jogee purakh oeh sundar naaree |
lines.english: He is a Yogi, and she is a beautiful woman.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਭੋਗੈ ਚੋਜ ਬਿਨੋਦੀ ਉਠਿ ਚਲਤੈ ਮਤਾ ਨ ਕੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: ahinis bhogai choj binodee utth chalatai mataa na keenaa he |6|
lines.english: Day and night, he enjoys her with delight, and then he arises and departs without consulting her. ||6||
Creating the Universe, God remains diffused throughout it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੂਆ ਡੋਲੈ ਦੂਤ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਸੋ ਪਾਏ ਜੋ ਕਿਛੁ ਕੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: manooaa ddolai doot sangat mil so paae jo kichh keenaa he |7|
lines.english: The mind wavers, keeping company with evil passions; one obtains the rewards of his own actions. ||7||
In the wind, water and fire, He vibrates and resounds.
When the order to depart is issued, how can he remain here?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਰਕ ਕੂਪ ਮਹਿ ਗੋਤੇ ਖਾਵੈ ਜਿਉ ਜਲ ਤੇ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਮੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੮॥
lines.translit: narak koop meh gote khaavai jiau jal te baahar meenaa he |8|
lines.english: He falls into the pit of hell, and suffers like a fish out of water. ||8||
Forgetting the Naam, one suffers the misery of his evil ways.
The faithless cynic has to endure 8.4 million hellish incarnations.
As he acts, so does he suffer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਬਾਝਹੁ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਈ ਕਿਰਤਿ ਬਾਧਾ ਗ੍ਰਸਿ ਦੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੯॥
lines.translit: satigur baajhahu mukat na hoee kirat baadhaa gras deenaa he |9|
lines.english: Without the True Guru, there is no liberation. Bound and gagged by his own actions, he is helpless. ||9||
This path is very narrow, like the sharp edge of a sword.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਤ ਪਿਤਾ ਕਲਤ੍ਰ ਸੁਤ ਬੇਲੀ ਨਾਹੀ ਬਿਨੁ ਹਰਿ ਰਸ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਕੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: maat pitaa kalatr sut belee naahee bin har ras mukat na keenaa he |10|
lines.english: Mother, father, spouse and child - none is anyone's friend in the end. Without the Lord's Love, no one is liberated. ||10||
When his account is read, he shall be crushed like the sesame seed in the mill.
but without the Guru, the Transcendent Lord Incarnate, there is no one at all.
You may have many friends and companions in the world,

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਪਰਾਇਣਿ ਅਨਦਿਨੁ ਕੀਰਤਨੁ ਕੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: gur kee sevaa mukat paraaein anadin keeratan keenaa he |11|
lines.english: Service to the Guru is the way to liberation. Night and day, sing the Kirtan of the Lord's Praises. ||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚ ਵਖਰ ਕੇ ਵਾਪਾਰੀ ਵਿਰਲੇ ਲੈ ਲਾਹਾ ਸਉਦਾ ਕੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: saach vakhar ke vaapaaree virale lai laahaa saudaa keenaa he |12|
lines.english: Very few are those who trade in the merchandise of Truth. Those who deal in it, obtain the true profit. ||12||
Abandon falsehood, and pursue the Truth,
and you shall obtain the fruits of your desires.
and you shall intuitively obtain the Blessed Vision of His Darshan, in the Mansion of His Presence.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਵਖਰੁ ਲੈ ਚਲਹੁ ॥
lines.translit: har har naam vakhar lai chalahu |
lines.english: Depart with the merchandise of the Name of the Lord, Har, Har,

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਖੋਜਿ ਲਹਹਿ ਜਨ ਪੂਰੇ ਇਉ ਸਮਦਰਸੀ ਚੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: guramukh khoj laheh jan poore iau samadarasee cheenaa he |13|
lines.english: The Gurmukhs search for Him and find Him; they are the perfect humble beings. In this way, they see Him, who looks upon all alike. ||13||
God is endless; following the Guru's Teachings, some find Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਬਾਣੀ ਸਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਮਾਨਹੁ ਇਉ ਆਤਮ ਰਾਮੈ ਲੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: satigur kee baanee sat sat kar maanahu iau aatam raamai leenaa he |14|
lines.english: Accept as True, Perfectly True, the Word of the True Guru's Bani. In this way, you shall merge in the Lord, the Supreme Soul. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸਬਦਿ ਮਨ ਕਉ ਸਮਝਾਵਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai sabad man kau samajhaaveh |
lines.english: Through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, they instruct their minds.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਤੇਰੀ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਤੂ ਸਿਰਿ ਸਿਰਿ ਦਾਤਾ ਸਭੁ ਤੇਰੋ ਕਾਰਣੁ ਕੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: sabh teree kudarat too sir sir daataa sabh tero kaaran keenaa he |15|
lines.english: Your creative power permeates all; You are the Great Giver of all. You created the whole creation. ||15||
Your servants are the greatest of the great, throughout the three worlds.
Naarad and Saraswati are Your servants.
Some serve at Your Door, and their sufferings are dispelled.
They are robed with honor in the Court of the Lord, and emancipated by the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਬੰਧਨ ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਤੋੜੇ ਚਿਤੁ ਚੰਚਲੁ ਚਲਣਿ ਨ ਦੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: haumai bandhan satigur torre chit chanchal chalan na deenaa he |16|
lines.english: The True Guru breaks the bonds of egotism, and restrains the fickle consciousness. ||16||
Meet the True Guru, and search for the way,

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਮਾਰਿ ਕਰਹੁ ਗੁਰ ਸੇਵਾ ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਹਰਿ ਰੰਗਿ ਭੀਨਾ ਹੇ ॥੧੭॥੨॥੮॥
lines.translit: haumai maar karahu gur sevaa jan naanak har rang bheenaa he |17|2|8|
lines.english: Subdue your egotism, and serve the Guru; O servant Nanak, you shall be drenched with the Lord's Love. ||17||2||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਪਾਵਹੁ ਗਣਤ ਨ ਕਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: jit prabh paavahu ganat na kaaee |
lines.english: by which you may find God, and not have to answer for your account.
My Lord is the Destroyer of demons.
My Beloved Lord is pervading each and every heart.
Maaroo, First Mehl:
The Holy Gurmukh seeks Your Sanctuary.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਲੇ ਅਲਖੁ ਨ ਲਖੀਐ ਮੂਲੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਲਿਖੁ ਵੀਚਾਰਾ ਹੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naale alakh na lakheeai moole guramukh likh veechaaraa he |1|
lines.english: The unseen Lord is always with us, but He is not seen at all. The Gurmukh contemplates the record. ||1||
